title: OpenHands
oneLiner: Open-source AI software development agent for coding tasks, repositories, and developer workflows.
summary: OpenHands is an open-source AI-driven development project for letting agents work on software tasks, inspect repositories, modify code, and support developer workflows.
whyItMatters: OpenHands matters because coding agents are moving from chat suggestions toward repository-level action. It gives developers an open implementation to inspect when evaluating how agents plan, edit, test, and recover inside real codebases.
bestFor: Teams that want an inspectable alternative to closed coding-agent products
Researchers studying repository-level task automation and software engineering agents
notFor: Non-technical users looking for a general personal assistant
Teams unwilling to review sandboxing and repository permissions before using coding agents
seoTitle: OpenHands: Open-source AI coding agent for developers
seoDescription: OpenHands profile: what the open-source coding agent is, when to use it, official links, command line, and OpenAgent structured data.
shareDescription: Open-source AI-driven development agent for coding workflows.

coreStrengths.title: Repository-level coding agent
coreStrengths.description: OpenHands is designed around software development tasks, not only answering programming questions.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Real coding assistance requires file edits, terminal work, testing, and iteration.

coreStrengths.title: Open implementation for agent workflows
coreStrengths.description: The public repository lets builders inspect how the system approaches planning, execution, and developer controls.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Coding agents touch valuable codebases, so inspectability matters.

coreStrengths.title: Strong fit for coding-agent comparison
coreStrengths.description: OpenHands is a useful reference point when comparing open coding agents against proprietary tools.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Teams can evaluate tradeoffs around local control, hosted convenience, and engineering safety.

useCaseNotes.title: Issue triage and code changes
useCaseNotes.description: Use OpenHands as a candidate when an agent needs to inspect a repository and propose implementation changes.

useCaseNotes.title: Coding-agent research
useCaseNotes.description: Study how an open coding agent handles tasks, tools, sandboxing, and developer feedback.

useCaseNotes.title: Self-hosted developer automation
useCaseNotes.description: Evaluate whether parts of your coding workflow can run in an inspectable open-source environment.

compareNotes.title: Choose OpenHands for software development workflows
compareNotes.summary: browser-use and OpenClaw are stronger for web workflows. OpenHands is more directly focused on repositories, code tasks, and software engineering.

commandLine.label: Clone the OpenHands repository
commandLine.description: Start with the official repository and follow the docs for the current Docker or runtime setup.

seoArticle.intro: OpenHands is a practical open-source reference point for the coding-agent category. It is not just a chat interface for code explanations; it is aimed at agents that work inside software development workflows.
seoArticle.whatItIs: OpenHands is an open-source AI-driven development project. It gives builders a way to evaluate repository-level agent behavior, including code inspection, edits, execution, and developer feedback loops.
seoArticle.whyItMatters: Coding agents need more trust than chatbots because they can change files, run commands, and touch repositories. OpenHands is useful because its implementation can be inspected, tested, and compared against closed coding-agent products.
seoArticle.howItWorks: A careful evaluation should start with a sandbox repository. Give the agent a narrow task, inspect its plan, watch the commands it runs, and review the resulting diff before expanding to more important codebases.

useCases.title: Open coding-agent benchmarking
useCases.description: Use OpenHands to test repository-level tasks and compare results with other coding agents.

useCases.title: Developer workflow automation
useCases.description: Evaluate whether repeated code maintenance tasks can be delegated safely under review.

useCases.title: Agent safety and sandboxing studies
useCases.description: Study the operational boundaries needed when agents can modify code and run commands.

alternatives.title: Use browser-use or OpenClaw for web actions
alternatives.summary: OpenHands is for software development workflows. Browser-oriented agents are better when the core task happens inside websites.

faq.question: Is OpenHands open source?
faq.answer: The project repository shows an MIT license badge in its README. Verify the repository license before production use.

faq.question: Is OpenHands only for developers?
faq.answer: Yes, its clearest fit is software development and repository workflows.

faq.question: Should I use OpenHands on production code immediately?
faq.answer: No. Start with a sandbox repository and review every change before moving to important code.
